title: Is the complacency in global financial markets warranted?
content: Financial markets so far have reflected the belief that the Iran war will be over soon and that commerce will return to normal. I explain why I think this belief is unwarranted and why President Trump's latest announcement regarding bombing Iran's power plants is economic suicide.
creator: Kurt Cobb
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Worse than 2008?
content: Several commentators have remarked that the United States’ war on Iran carries echoes of 2008. A potential financial crash this year could actually be much worse.
creator: Richard Heinberg
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Finding Home Part II: Water
content: It is time to examine how we live, where we live, where we draw the line. We can take our anxiety and move it into action. We can plan and protect ourselves. And, amazingly, wonderfully, saving rainwater is something every one of us can do. It's time.
creator: Zia Gallina
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: How “Fire Fungi” Help Put Burnt Landscapes Back Together
content: Before plants and animals recolonize after a wildfire, fungi get to work.
creator: Hannah Thomasy
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: (Part 2) A Review of Scott Galloway’s “Notes on Being a Man”: Masculinity and the Landscape of the Soul
content: The question is no longer just how to succeed in the world. It is how to remain human in a time of unraveling, and how to become, in the deepest sense, both soulful and revolutionary: ruthless in understanding the material conditions of the age, yet still capable of love, grief, reverence, and fidelity to life.
creator: Brad Hornick
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Finding Lights in a Dark Age: Excerpt
content: If we and our descendants succeed in realising this new green Earth, I think it will result from ordinary people sharing and distributing what they need locally to generate renewable communities oriented to practical livelihood.
creator: Chris Smaje
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: ECLIPSE: Living in the Shadow of Injustice
content: The eclipse, then, is an invitation. Not a warning or a demand, but a quiet reminder that clarity is possible. Justice has not abandoned us. We stepped out of alignment with it, and we can step back. The shadow will stay as long as we remain in it, but it will fade the moment we stop standing in it.
creator: Don Christoff
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Paul Ehrlich: A Tribute
content: While The Population Bomb is the book with which Ehrlich is most closely identified, he wrote dozens of others, including important and fascinating works on birds, human ecology, and conservation biology. He was as insightful as he was prolific, and his work deserves continued attention.
creator: Richard Heinberg
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: Notes on Being a Man: Review
content: The challenge before us is therefore larger than "teaching men how to succeed". It is to teach them how to succeed without destroying the world that makes success meaningful and, when necessary, how to transform the systems that place those goals in conflict.
creator: Brad Hornick
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: The whole food system must be converted – not just the farming system
content: It is essential to re-connect food to the land and the process of farming. This will give food an enhanced value not only as a supplier of energy and essential nutrients, but also as a source of meaning and experience of the land, of the living and of the people producing food.
creator: Gunnar Rundgren
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.

title: After Loneliness
content: The news hasn’t improved since I started working on this article. Still, while doing so, I’ve found myself in the company of others — and that’s reminded me of something. When you make yourself go out into the world, however scary it might seem, and act to make it better, the world does sometimes shift. The atoms really do move.
creator: Mattea Kramer
sourceDescription: Insight and inspiration in turbulent times.
